g.data-narration: Welcome to Software Estimation. Learn how to predict effort, time, and cost before building software.
Software estimation predicts effort, time, and cost. Poor estimation causes project failures, cost overruns, and delays.
g.text: • Critical for project planning and resource allocation
• Poor estimation is a primary cause of project failures
• Enables risk management and client satisfaction

g.data-narration: We will explore size estimation methods like LOC and Function Points, the COCOMO model, and estimation accuracy.
Size estimation is the foundation. LOC counts source code lines; Function Points measure user-perceived functionality.
g.text: Direct measure of source code size

g.data-narration: Function Points count five components: External Inputs, Outputs, Inquiries, Internal Logical Files, and External Interface Files.
Each component has complexity weights: Low, Average, and High. These determine the Unadjusted Function Points.
To calculate Unadjusted Function Points, multiply each component count by its complexity weight and sum all values.
g.text: Example: 10 EIs at Average complexity = 10 × 4 = 40 FP

g.data-narration: The Value Adjustment Factor accounts for 14 complexity characteristics. VAF ranges from 0.65 to 1.35.
g.text: Fi = 14 complexity adjustment factors rated 0-5 each

g.data-narration: Adjusted Function Points equal UFP multiplied by VAF. This gives the final FP count reflecting project complexity.
Let's calculate FP for a system with 12 EIs, 8 EOs, 5 ILFs, and 3 EIFs. UFP equals 155, and with VAF of 1.07, AFP is 166.
The Constructive Cost Model (COCOMO) by Barry Boehm provides formulas to estimate effort and development time based on code size.
g.text: KLOC = Thousands of Lines of Code

g.data-narration: COCOMO defines three project types: Organic (simple), Semi-detached (medium), and Embedded (complex with hardware constraints).
For an embedded system with 25 KLOC, effort equals 136 person-months, and development time is about 14 months.
For the same 10 KLOC, an embedded project needs 57 person-months versus 27 for organic - more than double the effort.
Early estimates have ±50% uncertainty. Accuracy improves as the project progresses. Use multiple techniques for better results.
Remember these essential formulas: UFP calculation, VAF formula, AFP calculation, and the COCOMO effort and time equations.
Function Points are language-independent and user-focused. COCOMO shows non-linear effort growth. Use multiple estimation techniques for best results.
g.text: ✓ FP counts user-perceived functionality, not code
✓ COCOMO b > 1 proves effort grows exponentially with size
✓ Embedded projects need more effort than organic
✓ VAF ranges 0.65 to 1.35 based on 14 factors
